evidence: The presence of solid yellow double center lines is consistent with Brazilian road marking standards on two-way roads.
Reddish-brown soil (Terra Roxa or Cerrado soil) is a primary indicator of agricultural regions in Brazil, particularly in Mato Grosso, Mato Grosso do Sul, and Paraná.
Vast flat agricultural fields or plains are characteristic of the Brazilian interior's large-scale soybean and corn production areas.
Lattice-style electricity transmission towers are standard infrastructure for high-voltage power distribution across rural Brazil.
The sighting of distant palm trees alongside agricultural plains is highly typical of the Cerrado biome in central Brazil.
